Extractor.reasoning: Hemophilia A is an X-linked recessive disorder. Since the father is affected (hemophilia A), he carries the mutation on his X chromosome. Therefore, each daughter has a 50% chance of being a carrier (inheriting the affected X from their father) and a 50% chance of being unaffected (inheriting the normal X from their mother). Since the second daughter is one of the three daughters, the probability remains 50%.
